text:
Pap (パプ, Papu) the "Human-Keeping Beast" (, Hitokai no Kemono) is a mysterious "beast" that feeds on humans and keeps them as pets, and is counted as one of the Five Threats. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 341 It lives in a mountain range in the Dark Continent along the northeast shore of Lake Mobius, but some of its victims have also been found in the known world. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 344 

Appearance

Pap's appearance is still unknown. It seems to be able to sprout feelers which it inserts in the skulls of its human victims. 

History

When Begerossé Union sent 1000 people into an expedition to the Dark Continent, they went through the north of Lake Mobius, and secured a route inside the rugged mountains. There they found a rare mineral called Unmanned Rock that once submerged in water, even with one small bead, it can generate 20,000 kilowatts of power for one entire day. Unfortunately for them, that zone was also the territory of Pap that destroyed them effortlessly, leaving only seven survivors. Some of its victims were also found in the known world, and are secretly stored in the basement of the International Permit Agency. 

Abilities & Powers

According to the "Dangerous Creatures Evaluation List" Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Extras utilized by the Hunter Association, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 346 Pap merits a threat level of A, which is even higher than that of the Chimera Ants at B. 

Its victims appear to be shrunk to the size of dolls, and all have a tube-like appendage sticking out of their head. The creature's full title reads "A trade-off between life and pleasure", which may be related to its abilities, and is said to be capable of taming humans into pets. 

Trivia

Since multiple sets of eyes were shown in Chapter 341, Pap might be a species rather than a single creature.
The Pap's ability to shrink victims is likely based on the tradition of creating shrunken heads, which has religious significance to some indigenous tribes. It is believed that shrinking the head of an enemy can harness their spirit and compel him to serve the shrinker.
